title: Personal Codex Subscription Auth And Hosted Proxy Tasks
principles: Interactive ChatGPT subscription login runs only on the trusted local owner machine through a loopback PKCE callback; Vercel exposes no OAuth browser routes.
This is a private single-owner personal integration, not a public or multi-user authentication product.
Persist only an encrypted versioned subscription profile; never persist the authorization code, PKCE verifier, state, raw response, cookie, or ID token.
Every profile mutation uses a fenced compare-and-set commit so an expired lease holder cannot overwrite a newer login, refresh, or reauthentication state.
Refresh under the distributed lock, re-read under lock, and perform one revision-aware forced refresh/replay after a classified 401.
Gateway remains the default Eve model path; Eve integration requires a separate SPEC.
Use Effect Schema, tagged errors, Context services, explicit Layers, Config, Redacted, Effect Platform HTTP and Command, Scope, Deferred, and KeyValueStore for runtime-owned boundaries.
Official OpenAI docs and openai/codex source are protocol authority; community repositories are personal interoperability precedent only.
Preview proof comes before any production decision, and production requires separate explicit user approval.
globalVerification: Revalidate current OpenAI authentication docs and openai/codex source protocol fields before live implementation and proof.
Sanitized local loopback login proof with no credentials, callback query, code, verifier, state, account id, or raw provider payload
Upstash readback proof of a versioned ciphertext envelope with no plaintext credential markers
Personal Vercel preview proof of proactive refresh, fenced rotation, concurrency, bounded 401 recovery, private SSE, safe failures, and leak-free logs
Dedicated final 3-pass Effect TS audit sweep with evidence

implementationImprovementAuditCounter.description: The parent records distinct ownership/call-graph, Effect implementation-quality, and verification-coverage passes before acceptance. Additional passes are required while any finding remains unresolved.

tasks.title: Revalidate personal Codex subscription authentication
tasks.description: Correct the hosted-redirect assumption using official docs, current openai/codex source research, and a pinned community reference.
implementationNotes: Official docs establish Sign in with ChatGPT for subscription access in local Codex clients.
Current openai/codex source is authoritative for loopback PKCE, transports, ports, scopes, token fields, account metadata, expiry, and refresh behavior.
The pinned opencode reference is interoperability evidence only and its unsafe JSON, logging, expires_in, and manual fallback patterns are rejected.
The accepted boundary is trusted-local login plus encrypted hosted profile sync; Vercel has no OAuth start/callback route.
mandatoryVerification: Record source categories without credential values or local auth-cache content.
Distinguish official evidence, community precedent, and Bundjil inference.
Verify local login, hosted refresh, test, proof, failure, and rollback call graphs.
jq empty docs/product-specs/codex-hosted-live-oauth-storage.tasks.json
git diff --check
completionCriteria: Implementation is not blocked on a Vercel redirect grant.
Personal/single-owner scope and product-support risk are explicit.

evidence: Parent audit 1 - ownership/call graph: interactive callback ownership moved to a trusted-local package command; Vercel retains encrypted profile use and refresh only.
Parent audit 2 - quality: revised contracts require scoped Effect Platform HTTP/Command, Deferred callback completion, Schema/Redacted boundaries, tagged failures, and no hosted state store/routes.

Parent audit 3 - evidence: official docs, Executor Personal DeepWiki on openai/codex, pinned opencode source inspection, current repo contracts, and prior preview proof were reconciled without reading credentials.

tasks.title: Implement encrypted storage and refresh locking
tasks.description: Retain the accepted encrypted KeyValueStore composition and atomic Upstash refresh lease as foundations for fenced mutation.
implementationNotes: Reuse encrypted storage, canonical subject hashes, atomic acquire, TTL, owner release, and lock-aware profile re-read.
The existing lease is coordination, not sufficient stale-write protection; the pending fenced-commit task completes that invariant.
mandatoryVerification: Flat named storage/lock Effects, typed errors, canonical Schemas/services, and no unsafe casts/DTOs/manual readers/helpers.
Encrypted save/read/remove, contention, expiry, owner release, and winner re-read tests.

completionCriteria: Encrypted persistence and refresh coordination remain accepted foundations.

evidence: Parent audit 1 - ownership/call graph: persistence/lock remain package-owned; Redis details stay in the adapter.

tasks.title: Define subscription profile variants and fenced commits
tasks.description: Add explicit legacy/import and refresh-capable subscription profile variants, opaque credential revisions, migration behavior, and atomic fenced profile mutation.
implementationNotes: CodexAccessTokenImportProfile represents legacy ciphertext; CodexSubscriptionProfile requires refresh token, account id, access-JWT expiry, protocol/scope version, revision, timestamps, and reauthentication state.
Decode existing V1 plaintext only as legacy and never silently treat it as refresh-capable.
Add CodexOAuthProfileCommit with Upstash fenced-CAS and memory layers; login, refresh, and permanent-failure marking all use it.
A stale lease holder or stale permanent failure receives a typed conflict and cannot mutate a newer revision.
Add sanitized refresh observer events/counters without exposing revision values.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen/Effect.fn decode, migrate, commit, conflict, and observer operations with typed .pipe(...) errors.
Canonical Effect Schema unions/brands and service contracts; no unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual readers/mappers, raw JSON, or trivial helpers.

Tests cover legacy decode, required subscription fields, V2 round trip, initial write, matching-revision commit, stale commit rejection, lease expiry during mutation, concurrent login replacement, stale reauth marking, and observer redaction.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth check-types
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth build
bun run verification
completionCriteria: No delayed refresh/login/failure can overwrite a newer profile generation.
Legacy profiles remain readable only as access-token fallback.

evidence: Parent audit 1 - ownership/call graph: explicit legacy/subscription variants and fenced mutation remain owned by @bundjil/codex-oauth; the Upstash commit layer is separately named, while current local/live proxy composition explicitly uses the unsupported commit layer until the later enablement task.

Parent audit 2 - implementation quality: reviewed canonical Schema unions/brands, Context services, explicit memory/Upstash Layers, flat named Effects, AES-GCM V1 migration/V2 encoding, atomic Lua create/CAS, shared atomic memory state, sanitized observer events, and restricted legacy putProfile; Ultracite and stale-pattern scans found no unsafe casts, raw JSON calls, process.env reads, duplicated operation schemas, or unresolved helper sprawl.

Parent audit 3 - verification coverage: focused tests prove V1 legacy-only migration, required subscription fields, V2 round trip, create-only writes, matching replacement/refresh, stale worker and reauthentication rejection, concurrent login replacement, observer redaction, and mocked Upstash winner preservation; package 60/60 tests, proxy 11/11 tests, package/proxy builds, mock SSE smoke, git diff --check, and bun run verification all passed.

tasks.title: Implement the trusted-local subscription login broker
tasks.description: Implement protocol config, loopback PKCE, browser launch, separate code-exchange/refresh transports, account/expiry extraction, fenced encrypted save, and sanitized CLI output.
implementationNotes: Add CodexSubscriptionAuthProtocolConfigService, CodexOAuthHttpClient, CodexLoopbackCallback, CodexBrowserLauncher, CodexSubscriptionLogin, and explicit live/test layers.
Use first-party ports 1455/1457, scope set, PKCE S256, state, strict callback validation, timeout, request-log suppression, and scoped cleanup for every exit.
Authorization URL/state/verifier/code/token payload/ID token stay redacted and memory-only; reveal URL only to the browser launcher.
Code exchange is form encoded; refresh is JSON; both responses use narrow Schemas and no expires_in assumption.
Derive expiry from access JWT and required account metadata from the current first-party canonical source; persist no ID token.
Exclude manual paste from the tracer unless separately proven safe.
mandatoryVerification: Flat named login/callback/exchange/metadata/profile-save/cleanup Effects with tagged .pipe(...) errors.
Canonical Schema types and no unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual readers/mappers, raw fetch/JSON, process.env package reads, or helper sprawl.

Tests cover protocol URL, ports, scopes, state entropy/mismatch, PKCE, callback method/path/denial/missing-code/timeout/conflict, exchange without expires_in, access-JWT expiry, account metadata, cross-account rejection, ID-token disposal, browser failure, every Scope finalizer path, fenced save, and sanitized output.
Automated proof uses mock providers only.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth check-types
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth build
bun run verification
completionCriteria: Mock loopback login writes a refresh-capable encrypted profile without hosted callback routes.
No app, Vercel, Eve, browser bundle, or CI imports local callback/browser layers.

tasks.title: Run the real local login tracer
tasks.description: Complete owner browser sign-in through localhost and prove a refresh-capable encrypted subscription profile reaches personal Upstash before hosted refresh work continues.
implementationNotes: Use the trusted local command and current first-party protocol; no Vercel callback.
Record status, mode, refresh-capable flag, profile variant, ciphertext shape, and leak booleans only.
Stop downstream tasks if login interoperability, account metadata, expiry, or encrypted persistence differs from the SPEC.
mandatoryVerification: Loopback sign-in completes and emits sanitized Schema output only.
Upstash readback is a V2 ciphertext envelope with no credential, code, verifier, state, account-id, callback, JWT-claim, or ID-token markers.
Stored profile decrypts only inside the trusted proof and confirms required subscription fields without printing values.
No credential/callback/provider payload appears in local logs or shell history.
bun run verification
completionCriteria: Real local subscription login and encrypted profile sync are proven before hosted refresh implementation.

tasks.title: Enable refresh-capable private live proxy mode
tasks.description: Implement proactive fenced refresh, atomic credentials, revision-aware 401 recovery, explicit app errors/readiness, and private live composition without OAuth browser routes.
implementationNotes: getValidCredential returns access token, account id, and revision atomically; it refreshes inside skew under lock and fenced-CAS commits rotation.
recoverAfterUnauthorized uses a newer winner or forces refresh of the unchanged rejected revision, then permits one replay only.
Permanent credential failures fenced-mark reauthentication; transient network/timeout/rate-limit/5xx failures do not mutate storage.
Account metadata is invariant across refresh unless current source proves a valid same-account update.

Refresh transport decodes optional provider fields, but success requires a new access token and valid JWT expiry; omitted refresh/ID tokens retain the current refresh token/account id only when current source permits it.
HTTP 502 codex_reauthentication_required, HTTP 503 codex_auth_temporarily_unavailable, sanitized provider 502, and non-ready live health are explicit.
No hosted OAuth route, API-key fallback, mock fallback, or retry loop.
mandatoryVerification: Flat named credential/refresh/commit/recovery/retry/route/readiness Effects with tagged .pipe(...) errors and canonical Schemas.
No unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual readers/mappers, raw JSON, process.env package reads, or helper sprawl.

Tests cover valid bypass, skew refresh, lock winner/re-read, rotated replacement, omitted access failure, allowed refresh/ID omission retention, malformed expiry, lease-expiry stale commit, concurrent login, transient no-mutation, permanent fenced mark, cross-account no-mutation, revision-aware forced refresh, one replay, second-401 stop, unrelated no-replay, legacy refusal, missing config, mock/local preservation, error codes, readiness, and leak safety.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth check-types
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy check-types
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy build
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-proxy smoke-test
bun run verification
completionCriteria: Mocked private route proves durable fenced refresh and bounded 401 recovery.
No stale writer can overwrite a newer credential generation.

tasks.title: Run hosted refresh and private proxy preview proof
tasks.description: Prove private personal preview streaming and safe refresh using isolated proof state and sanitized refresh observation.
implementationNotes: Use an isolated proof subject/namespace and do not damage the owner's working profile to simulate permanent failure.
Record statuses, mode, ciphertext shape, content type, SSE count/DONE, refresh winner/follower booleans, and leak booleans only.
Permanent failure is deterministic/mock proof; real preview proves normal proactive/concurrent refresh and private streaming.
Do not deploy production.
mandatoryVerification: Live health is ready; unauthenticated/invalid-token calls are 401; authenticated SSE is 200 with data and DONE.
Near-expiry concurrent requests produce one safe observed refresh winner, followers use the winner, revision changes once, and no stale overwrite occurs.
Upstash remains a ciphertext envelope with no credential/callback/account/JWT markers.
Deterministic permanent failure yields 502 reauthentication; transient failure yields 503 and preserves profile; second 401 does not retry.
Vercel logs/responses contain no credentials, revision values, callback query, provider payload, prompt, request body, or full model response.
bun run verification
completionCriteria: Personal preview has a durable refresh-capable proof with sanitized evidence.
Production remains a separate explicit decision.

tasks.title: Run the mandatory 3-pass Effect TS audit sweep
tasks.description: Perform the cross-cutting ownership, Effect quality, and verification audit before any production proposal.
implementationNotes: Pass 1 verifies package/app/local-command ownership and production/test/CLI/proof/rollback call graphs.

Pass 2 audits flat Effects, Context services, explicit Layers, Effect Platform resource scoping, Deferred callback, Schema types, tagged errors, Config.redacted, final-boundary Redacted access, fenced persistence, and absence of unsafe casts/DTOs/manual readers/raw JSON/process.env/stringly tags/helper sprawl.
Pass 3 proves targeted/root checks, local login, encrypted readback, hosted rotation/concurrency, bounded retry, SSE, errors/readiness, Vercel leak scans, docs, and rollback.
Fix findings and add passes until no unresolved gap remains; production requires separate approval.
mandatoryVerification: All prior tasks have complete parent audit evidence.
No OAuth browser route exists in Vercel and no local callback/browser layer is imported by apps.
